title: Conversation Practice
description: Generate reusable phrases and follow-up replies from scenario, tone, and fluency level.
noticeDescription: This pack is tuned for {{scene}}, with a {{tone}} tone at {{level}} difficulty.

travel.friendly: Hi, I want to make sure I take the fastest route to downtown today. Could you help me check?
travel.professional: Hello, I want to confirm the timing and arrangement for this trip. Could you help me verify it?
travel.calm: Hi, I want to ask about the route and timing.

meeting.friendly: Hi everyone, let me quickly share the current progress and then we can align on next steps together.
meeting.professional: Hello everyone, I will start with the current status and then outline the decisions we need.
meeting.calm: I will cover the current status first, then the points that need support.

daily.friendly: How have you been lately? I just thought of something and wanted to hear your take.
daily.professional: I have an idea and wanted to take a few minutes to confirm it with you.
daily.calm: I want to talk briefly about this.

travel.1: Checking in or asking about facilities
travel.2: Ordering food or adjusting a request

friendly.0: I just want to confirm first whether we can handle it this way.
friendly.1: If it is convenient, could you help me check this option one more time?
friendly.2: That sounds good. I also want to add one small idea.

professional.0: To keep us aligned, I want to confirm the current assumption first.
professional.1: There is a risk here, and I suggest we align on the handling approach first.
professional.2: Based on the current information, I lean toward the safer option.

calm.0: I want to confirm one thing first.
calm.1: There may be an issue here.

beginner.0: If the other person answers quickly, repeat the key word first to buy time.
beginner.1: If you miss something, ask them to slow down or repeat it. Do not force it.

intermediate.0: Repeat the key point before answering to sound more natural and steady.
intermediate.1: If they disagree, acknowledge it first and then explain your reasoning.
intermediate.2: Add one reason sentence to make the response feel complete.

advanced.0: Use your answer to move the conversation toward the next step.
advanced.1: In disagreement, use a clarifying question to bring the discussion back to facts.
advanced.2: Close with a confirmation line so both sides leave with the same expectation.
